_about: Editable error catalog for the Entra Connect sync health monitor. 'events' maps Windows Application-log event IDs (sources ADSync / Directory Synchronization) to a meaning and remediation checklist. 'special' holds non-event-log detection keys. 'unmapped' is the fallback for any event ID not listed here -- unmapped events are still logged and alerted, never dropped. Event IDs 0/611/652/655/6900 are documented on Microsoft Learn (troubleshoot-pwd-sync, tshoot-connect-password-hash-synchronization). Add new IDs here as they are encountered; no code change is needed.

0.meaning: Directory Synchronization connectivity/credential error (documented on Microsoft Learn alongside 611/652/655 as a password-sync connectivity problem; one known variant is Entra credentials invalidated after being updated through FIM).
checklist: Read the full event message -- ID 0 is a catch-all; the message text identifies the actual failure.
Check connectivity from the sync server to Microsoft Entra (proxy/firewall changes).
If the message says the user name or password is incorrect, re-run the Microsoft Entra Connect configuration wizard to refresh the connector credentials.

611.meaning: Password hash synchronization failed for a domain (DRS/RPC replication error against a domain controller, e.g. RPC 8453 'Replication access was denied').
checklist: Identify the failing domain from the event message.
Verify the AD DS Connector account has 'Replicating Directory Changes' and 'Replicating Directory Changes All' at the root of that domain.
Check reachability of that domain's domain controllers from the sync server.
If errors persist, confirm Entra Connect is on a current build (several 611 variants are fixed bugs).

652.meaning: Failed credential provisioning batch -- password hash sync could not deliver a batch of password updates to Microsoft Entra ID.
checklist: Read the embedded error code (e.g. 90 = password sync not activated for the tenant; 81 = service busy/transient).
If error code 90: verify password hash sync is still enabled in Entra Connect (Get-ADSyncAADPasswordSyncState).
If transient (service busy): confirm it self-recovers on the next cycle before acting.

655.meaning: Failed credential provisioning ping -- the every-30-minutes password-sync heartbeat to Microsoft Entra ID failed.
checklist: Read the embedded error code and description in the event message.
If it reports invalid credentials, re-run the Entra Connect configuration wizard to refresh the connector account.
If it reports password sync not activated, re-enable password hash synchronization via the wizard.

6900.meaning: The server hit an unexpected error while processing a password change notification (commonly: password sync not activated for the tenant, or stale Entra connector credentials).
checklist: Read the embedded error code (90 = password sync not activated; credential errors name the account).
Re-enable password hash synchronization or re-run the configuration wizard as the message indicates.
Confirm subsequent password changes sync (check for follow-up 6900s after the fix).

STALE_SYNC.meaning: Microsoft Entra reports no successful sync from on-premises within the configured staleness threshold (tenant-side heartbeat, independent of what the local sync engine believes).
checklist: Check the ADSync service is running on the sync server (Get-Service ADSync).
Check the sync scheduler: Get-ADSyncScheduler (SyncCycleEnabled, NextSyncCycleStartTimeInUTC, staging mode).
Check Synchronization Service Manager for stopped/errored run profiles.
Check outbound connectivity from the sync server to Microsoft Entra.
If a maintenance window or deliberate pause explains it, note it and clear the alert.

HEARTBEAT_CHECK_FAILED.meaning: The monitor itself could not complete the Graph staleness check (auth failure, network, Graph outage) -- sync staleness is currently UNVERIFIED, not necessarily broken.
checklist: Read the error detail in this entry -- auth errors point at the app registration/secret, network errors at connectivity.
Check whether the client secret for the monitoring app registration has expired.
Confirm outbound HTTPS from the sync server to graph.microsoft.com.
Until this clears, treat sync staleness as unknown and verify manually in the Entra admin center.

unmapped.meaning: Unmapped ADSync/Directory Synchronization event -- not yet in the error catalog. Review manually.
checklist: Read the full event message in the Application event log (the RecordId is in this entry).
Search Microsoft Learn / Entra Connect troubleshooting docs for this event ID.
If it recurs, add the ID to error-catalog.json with a meaning and checklist.
